_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of local specialized system requirements (e.g., critical infrastructure systems that may not use standard information technology [IT]) for safety, performance, and reliability.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data classification, sensitivity labeling, and the architectural controls required to enforce data access, privacy, and residency requirements by design.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-K-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-A-001 (TF, cosine: 0.544), AG-K-013 (AG, cosine: 0.542).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.3/10 (raw 0.333). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge, requirements]. DCWF-only terms [critical, information, infrastructure, local, performance, reliability] vs WIDAI-only terms [access, architectural, classification, controls, data, design] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.868 (P=0.856, R=0.880). R exceeds P by 0.024 — the WIDAI KSA's 21 words are well-captured by the DCWF element's 23 words, but the DCWF element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Knowledge of local specialized system requirements (e.g., critical infrastructur" and "Knowledge of data classification, sensitivity labeling, and the architectural co" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.545 > STS 0.333 (gap +0.212). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge, requirements] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 10.3%. Shared tokens: [knowledge, requirements]. DCWF-only: [critical, information, infrastructure, local, performance, reliability]. WIDAI-only: [access, architectural, classification, controls, data, design].
